nodes.title: Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'Post Layering'
Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'Post Layering'

IMPORTANT!!!

The layout of every Animation Graph should ideally be determined by the specific needs of each individual project. Therefore, it is most likely necessary to modify parts (or all) of this Animation Graph and the Linked Layer Graphs in order to meet your project's specific needs. Thankfully, due to the highly flexible nature of UE4's animation system, it is entirely possible to keep/rearrange parts of these graphs (or concepts of this system) while also using a completely different setup.
nodes.comment:
IMPORTANT!!!

The layout of every Animation Graph should ideally be determined by the specific needs of each individual project. Therefore, it is most likely necessary to modify parts (or all) of this Animation Graph and the Linked Layer Graphs in order to meet your project's specific needs. Thankfully, due to the highly flexible nature of UE4's animation system, it is entirely possible to keep/rearrange parts of these graphs (or concepts of this system) while also using a completely different setup.

nodes.title: In Ragdoll a Blend Out Pose
Blend Out Pose a In Ragdoll
Transition to the Blend Out is 0 making the charatcer immediately assume the ragdoll's pose when blending back to the animation states,

nodes.title:
Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Interp the Aiming Rotation value to achieve smooth aiming rotation changes. Interpolating the rotation before calculating the angle ensures the value is not affected by changes in actor rotation, allowing slow aiming rotation changes with fast actor rotation changes.
nodes.comment: Interp the Aiming Rotation value to achieve smooth aiming rotation changes. Interpolating the rotation before calculating the angle ensures the value is not affected by changes in actor rotation, allowing slow aiming rotation changes with fast actor rotation changes.

nodes.title: Calculate the Aiming angle and Smoothed Aiming Angle by getting the delta between the aiming rotation and the actor rotation.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Aiming angle and Smoothed Aiming Angle by getting the delta between the aiming rotation and the actor rotation.

nodes.title: Use the Aiming Yaw Angle divided by the number of spine+pelvis bones to get the amount of spine rotation needed to remain facing the camera direction.
nodes.comment: Use the Aiming Yaw Angle divided by the number of spine+pelvis bones to get the amount of spine rotation needed to remain facing the camera direction.

nodes.title: Get the delta between the Movement Input rotation and Actor rotation and map it to a range of 0-1. This value is used in the aim offset behavior to make the character look toward the Movement Input.
nodes.comment: Get the delta between the Movement Input rotation and Actor rotation and map it to a range of 0-1. This value is used in the aim offset behavior to make the character look toward the Movement Input.

nodes.title: Separate the Aiming Yaw Angle into 3 separate Yaw Times. These 3 values are used in the Aim Offset behavior to improve the blending of the aim offset when rotating completely around the character. This allows you to keep the aiming responsive but still smoothly blend from left to right or right to left.
nodes.comment: Separate the Aiming Yaw Angle into 3 separate Yaw Times. These 3 values are used in the Aim Offset behavior to improve the blending of the aim offset when rotating completely around the character. This allows you to keep the aiming responsive but still smoothly blend from left to right or right to left.

nodes.title: Clamp the Aiming Pitch Angle to a range of 1 to 0 for use in the vertical aim sweeps.
nodes.comment: Clamp the Aiming Pitch Angle to a range of 1 to 0 for use in the vertical aim sweeps.

nodes.title: Set the Additive amount weights for each body part
nodes.comment: Set the Additive amount weights for each body part

nodes.title: Get the Aim Offset weight by getting the opposite of the Aim Offset Mask.
nodes.comment: Get the Aim Offset weight by getting the opposite of the Aim Offset Mask.

nodes.title: Blend and set the Hand IK weights to ensure they only are weighted if allowed by the Arm layers.
nodes.comment: Blend and set the Hand IK weights to ensure they only are weighted if allowed by the Arm layers.

nodes.title: Set whether the arms should blend in mesh space or local space. The Mesh space weight will always be 1 unless the Local Space (LS) curve is fully weighted.
nodes.comment: Set whether the arms should blend in mesh space or local space. The Mesh space weight will always be 1 unless the Local Space (LS) curve is fully weighted.

Set the Yaw Offsets. These values influence the "YawOffset" curve in the animgraph and are used to offset the characters rotation for more natural movement. The curves allow for fine control over how the offset behaves for each movement direction.
nodes.comment: Set the Yaw Offsets. These values influence the "YawOffset" curve in the animgraph and are used to offset the characters rotation for more natural movement. The curves allow for fine control over how the offset behaves for each movement direction.

nodes.title: Update the fall speed. Setting this value only while in the air allows you to use it within the AnimGraph for the landing strength. If not, the Z velocity would return to 0 on landing.
nodes.comment: Update the fall speed. Setting this value only while in the air allows you to use it within the AnimGraph for the landing strength. If not, the Z velocity would return to 0 on landing.

Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Calculate the Velocity Blend. This value represents the velocity amount of the actor in each direction (normalized so that diagonals equal .5 for each direction), and is used in a BlendMulti node to produce better directional blending than a standard blendspace.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Velocity Blend. This value represents the velocity amount of the actor in each direction (normalized so that diagonals equal .5 for each direction), and is used in a BlendMulti node to produce better directional blending than a standard blendspace.

nodes.title:
Get Float Value
La destinazione è Curve Float

Calculate the Diagnal Scale Amount. This value is used to scale the Foot IK Root bone to make the Foot IK bones cover more distance on the diagonal blends. Without scaling, the feet would not move far enough on the diagonal direction due to the linear translational blending of the IK bones. The curve is used to easily map the value.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Diagnal Scale Amount. This value is used to scale the Foot IK Root bone to make the Foot IK bones cover more distance on the diagonal blends. Without scaling, the feet would not move far enough on the diagonal direction due to the linear translational blending of the IK bones. The curve is used to easily map the value.

nodes.title:
Get Max Acceleration
La destinazione è Componente movimento personaggio

Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Get Actor Rotation
La destinazione è Actor

Get Max Braking Deceleration
La destinazione è Componente movimento personaggio

Calculate the Relative Acceleration Amount. This value represents the current amount of acceleration / deceleration relative to the actor rotation. It is normalized to a range of -1 to 1 so that -1 equals the Max Braking Deceleration, and 1 equals the Max Acceleration of the Character Movement Component.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Relative Acceleration Amount. This value represents the current amount of acceleration / deceleration relative to the actor rotation. It is normalized to a range of -1 to 1 so that -1 equals the Max Braking Deceleration, and 1 equals the Max Acceleration of the Character Movement Component.

nodes.title: Calculate the Walk Run Blend. This value is used within the Blendspaces to blend between walking and running.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Walk Run Blend. This value is used within the Blendspaces to blend between walking and running.

nodes.title:
Get Curve Value
La destinazione è Istanza animazione

Calculate the Stride Blend. This value is used within the blendspaces to scale the stride (distance feet travel) so that the character can walk or run at different movement speeds. It also allows the walk or run gait animations to blend independently while still matching the animation speed to the movement speed, preventing the character from needing to play a half walk+half run blend. The curves are used to map the stride amount to the speed for maximum control.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Stride Blend. This value is used within the blendspaces to scale the stride (distance feet travel) so that the character can walk or run at different movement speeds. It also allows the walk or run gait animations to blend independently while still matching the animation speed to the movement speed, preventing the character from needing to play a half walk+half run blend. The curves are used to map the stride amount to the speed for maximum control.

Get Float Value
La destinazione è Curve Float

Get Owning Component
La destinazione è Istanza animazione

Get World Scale
La destinazione è Componente di scena

Calculate the Play Rate by dividing the Character's speed by the Animated Speed for each gait. The lerps are determined by the "Weight_Gait" anim curve that exists on every locomotion cycle so that the play rate is always in sync with the currently blended animation. The value is also divided by the Stride Blend and the mesh scale so that the play rate increases as the stride or scale gets smaller.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Play Rate by dividing the Character's speed by the Animated Speed for each gait. The lerps are determined by the "Weight_Gait" anim curve that exists on every locomotion cycle so that the play rate is always in sync with the currently blended animation. The value is also divided by the Stride Blend and the mesh scale so that the play rate increases as the stride or scale gets smaller.

La destinazione è Istanza animazione

Get World Scale
La destinazione è Componente di scena

Calculate the Crouching Play Rate by dividing the Character's speed by the Animated Speed. This value needs to be separate from the standing play rate to improve the blend from crocuh to stand while in motion.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Crouching Play Rate by dividing the Character's speed by the Animated Speed. This value needs to be separate from the standing play rate to improve the blend from crocuh to stand while in motion.

nodes.title: Calculate the Movement Direction. This value represents the direction the character is moving relative to the camera during the Looking Cirection / Aiming rotation modes, and is used in the Cycle Blending Anim Layers to blend to the appropriate directional states.
nodes.comment: Calculate the Movement Direction. This value represents the direction the character is moving relative to the camera during the Looking Cirection / Aiming rotation modes, and is used in the Cycle Blending Anim Layers to blend to the appropriate directional states.

Step 1: Trace downward from the foot location to find the geometry. If the surface is walkable, save the Impact Location and Normal.
nodes.comment: Step 1: Trace downward from the foot location to find the geometry. If the surface is walkable, save the Impact Location and Normal.

nodes.title: Step 1.1: Find the difference in location from the Impact point and the expected (flat) floor location. These values are offset by the nomrmal multiplied by the foot height to get better behavior on angled surfaces.
nodes.comment: Step 1.1: Find the difference in location from the Impact point and the expected (flat) floor location. These values are offset by the nomrmal multiplied by the foot height to get better behavior on angled surfaces.

nodes.title: Step 2: Interp the Current Location Offset to the new target value. Interpolate at different speeds based on whether the new target is above or below the current one.
nodes.comment: Step 2: Interp the Current Location Offset to the new target value. Interpolate at different speeds based on whether the new target is above or below the current one.

nodes.title: Step 3: Interp the Current Rotation Offset to the new target value.
nodes.comment: Step 3: Interp the Current Rotation Offset to the new target value.

nodes.title: Step 1: Set Local FootLock Curve value

nodes.title: Step 2: Only update the FootLock Alpha if the new value is less than the current, or it equals 1. This makes it so that the foot can only blend out of the locked position or lock to a new position, and never blend in.
nodes.comment: Step 2: Only update the FootLock Alpha if the new value is less than the current, or it equals 1. This makes it so that the foot can only blend out of the locked position or lock to a new position, and never blend in.

nodes.title: Step 3: If the Foot Lock curve equals 1, save the new lock location and rotation in component space.
nodes.comment: Step 3: If the Foot Lock curve equals 1, save the new lock location and rotation in component space.

nodes.title: Step 4: If the Foot Lock Alpha has a weight, update the Foot Lock offsets to keep the foot planted in place while the capsule moves.

nodes.title: Get the distance traveled between frames relative to the mesh rotation to find how much the foot should be offset to remain planted on the ground.

nodes.title: Subtract the location difference from the current local location and rotate it by the rotation difference to keep the foot planted in component space.

nodes.comment: Subtract the Rotation Difference from the current Local Rotation to get the new local rotation.

Only perform a Turn In Place check if the character is looking toward the camera in Third Person, and if the "Enable Transition" curve is fully weighted. The Enable_Transition curve is modified within certain states of the AnimBP so that the character can only turn while in those states.

nodes.title:
Turn in Place
La destinazione è ALS Anim BP

Step 1: Check if Aiming angle is outside of the Turn Check Min Angle, and if the Aim Yaw Rate is below the Aim Yaw Rate Limit. If so, begin counting the Elapsed Delay Time. If not, reset the Elapsed Delay Time. This ensures the conditions remain true for a sustained peroid of time before turning in place.
nodes.comment: Step 1: Check if Aiming angle is outside of the Turn Check Min Angle, and if the Aim Yaw Rate is below the Aim Yaw Rate Limit. If so, begin counting the Elapsed Delay Time. If not, reset the Elapsed Delay Time. This ensures the conditions remain true for a sustained peroid of time before turning in place.

nodes.title: Step 2: Check if the Elapsed Delay time exceeds the set delay (mapped to the turn angle range). If so, trigger a Turn In Place.
nodes.comment: Step 2: Check if the Elapsed Delay time exceeds the set delay (mapped to the turn angle range). If so, trigger a Turn In Place.

Calculate the land prediction weight by tracing in the velocity direction to find a walkable surface the character is falling toward, and getting the 'Time' (range of 0-1, 1 being maximum, 0 being about to land) till impact. The Land Prediction Curve is used to control how the time affects the final weight for a smooth blend.
nodes.comment: Calculate the land prediction weight by tracing in the velocity direction to find a walkable surface the character is falling toward, and getting the 'Time' (range of 0-1, 1 being maximum, 0 being about to land) till impact. The Land Prediction Curve is used to control how the time affects the final weight for a smooth blend.

Use the relative Velocity direction and amount to determine how much the character should lean while in air. The Lean In Air curve gets the Fall Speed and is used as a multiplier to smoothly reverse the leaning direction when transitioning from moving upwards to moving downwards.
nodes.comment: Use the relative Velocity direction and amount to determine how much the character should lean while in air. The Lean In Air curve gets the Fall Speed and is used as a multiplier to smoothly reverse the leaning direction when transitioning from moving upwards to moving downwards.

nodes.title: Step 1: Check if the character should rotate left or right by checking if the Aiming Angle exceeds the threshold.
nodes.comment: Step 1: Check if the character should rotate left or right by checking if the Aiming Angle exceeds the threshold.

nodes.title: Step 2: If the character should be rotating, set the Rotate Rate to scale with the Aim Yaw Rate. This makes the character rotate faster when moving the camera faster.
nodes.comment: Step 2: If the character should be rotating, set the Rotate Rate to scale with the Aim Yaw Rate. This makes the character rotate faster when moving the camera faster.

La destinazione è Istanza animazione

Play Dynamic Transition
La destinazione è ALS Anim BP

Play Dynamic Transition
La destinazione è ALS Anim BP

Check each foot to see if the location difference between the IK_Foot bone and its desired / target location (determined via a virtual bone) exceeds a threshold. If it does, play an additive transition animation on that foot. The currently set transition plays the second half of a 2 foot transition animation, so that only a single foot moves. Because only the IK_Foot bone can be locked, the separate virtual bone allows the system to know its desired location when locked.
nodes.comment: Check each foot to see if the location difference between the IK_Foot bone and its desired / target location (determined via a virtual bone) exceeds a threshold. If it does, play an additive transition animation on that foot. The currently set transition plays the second half of a 2 foot transition animation, so that only a single foot moves. Because only the IK_Foot bone can be locked, the separate virtual bone allows the system to know its desired location when locked.

If the character stops while a foot is planted down, lock the correct foot.
nodes.comment: If the character stops while a foot is planted down, lock the correct foot.

nodes.title: If the character stops while a foot is NOT planted down, play a section of the cycle where the foot IS down, and lock it.
nodes.comment: If the character stops while a foot is NOT planted down, play a section of the cycle where the foot IS down, and lock it.

nodes.title: Choose whether a foot is planted or about to plant when stopping using the 'Feet_Position' anim curve. A value <.5 means the foot is planted, and a value >.5 means the foot is still in the air.
nodes.comment: Choose whether a foot is planted or about to plant when stopping using the 'Feet_Position' anim curve. A value <.5 means the foot is planted, and a value >.5 means the foot is still in the air.

nodes.title: The 'Feet_Position' curve also determines which foot is planted (or about to plant). Positive values mean the right foot is planted, negative values mean the left.
nodes.comment: The 'Feet_Position' curve also determines which foot is planted (or about to plant). Positive values mean the right foot is planted, negative values mean the left.

nodes.title: Additive stopping transitions are triggered via the "Entered State Event" on these states.
nodes.comment: Additive stopping transitions are triggered via the "Entered State Event" on these states.

Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'Overlay Layer Input'
Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'Overlay Layer Input'

The 'Layering Override Group' Slots are a great place to play additional upper body animations, like reloads, equips, etc. By using a single montage, you can apply an animation in different ways on different body parts by using the different slots. For example, you could play a reload animation on the arms, and play an additive version of the reload on the body so that it can be used in any stance. Just remember to include the 'Curves' slot so you can control how the curves get blended in.
nodes.comment: The 'Layering Override Group' Slots are a great place to play additional upper body animations, like reloads, equips, etc. By using a single montage, you can apply an animation in different ways on different body parts by using the different slots. For example, you could play a reload animation on the arms, and play an additive version of the reload on the body so that it can be used in any stance. Just remember to include the 'Curves' slot so you can control how the curves get blended in.

nodes.title: Play a normal or additive anim in the 'Curves' slot when playing a Layering Override animation to override or influence the layering curve values.
nodes.comment: Play a normal or additive anim in the 'Curves' slot when playing a Layering Override animation to override or influence the layering curve values.

nodes.title: Both a mesh space and local space version of the Base Additive is created so that different body parts can recieve different additive types. Local space works best on the arms so that the motion respects the rotation induced by the spine.
nodes.comment: Both a mesh space and local space version of the Base Additive is created so that different body parts can recieve different additive types. Local space works best on the arms so that the motion respects the rotation induced by the spine.

nodes.title: The extra 'Hands' layer is used to prevent additive motion on the arms from playing on the fingers and ik gun / object bones if the character is holding something.
nodes.comment: The extra 'Hands' layer is used to prevent additive motion on the arms from playing on the fingers and ik gun / object bones if the character is holding something.

nodes.title: Create layers from different body sections.
nodes.comment: Create layers from different body sections.

nodes.title: Although these layer nodes have no bones, they still influences the curve values. This allows the system to take the values directly from the curves present in the Base and Overlay Layer, preventing them from being affected by all the previous layering. If this is not done, it would be nearly impossible to control how the curves interact with each other, since they drive the blend amounts in the layering process.
nodes.comment: Although these layer nodes have no bones, they still influences the curve values. This allows the system to take the values directly from the curves present in the Base and Overlay Layer, preventing them from being affected by all the previous layering. If this is not done, it would be nearly impossible to control how the curves interact with each other, since they drive the blend amounts in the layering process.

nodes.title: Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'F Input'
Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'Sprint Input'
Usa posa memorizzata nella cache 'F Input'
Cache all directions + Sprint so they can be used in multiple places but only evaluate once.
nodes.comment: Cache all directions + Sprint so they can be used in multiple places but only evaluate once.

nodes.title: Apply Sprinting if not masked. The mask is used in the additive landing animations to improve the sprinting land. If not masked, the knees had the tendency to overcompress and cause IK issues.
nodes.comment: Apply Sprinting if not masked. The mask is used in the additive landing animations to improve the sprinting land. If not masked, the knees had the tendency to overcompress and cause IK issues.

nodes.title: This state machine blends the directional cycles together based on the movement direction and the 'HipOrientation_Bias' curve in order to achieve superior blending when switching directions or changing hip rotation. The L->R / F->B (center) transitions also trigger pivots via a notify event.
nodes.comment: This state machine blends the directional cycles together based on the movement direction and the 'HipOrientation_Bias' curve in order to achieve superior blending when switching directions or changing hip rotation. The L->R / F->B (center) transitions also trigger pivots via a notify event.
